title: ［柒出國際］Gphone 推咗兩隻英國犬上當哋報紙

replies.msg:
【英藉黑警返到家鄉點見鄉親父老呢？】

昨日接受英國《泰晤士報》訪問提到，兩位鎮壓香港抗爭既指揮官 —— 其實都係英國人。

Rupert Dover 同埋 David Jordan 兩位威到返祖家，相信佢地喺英國既親戚朋友，今日掀到國際版都一定見到，真係恭喜曬 🤗🤗🤗

兩位嚟自英國，有享受民主自由既機會，但嚟到香港既時候，就去鎮壓一班爭取民主自由既香港人，如今揚威祖家，你地都有今日啦！

#外國勢力可恥
#英帝忘我之心不死
#不許洋人說三道四
#係咪八國聯軍

“These officers come from Britain, a country where human rights, basic freedoms, justice and rule of law are taken for granted by its people,” he told The Sunday Times. “But that is what people here were demonstrating for when the police attacked them.”

----------

[Protesters turn ire on British officers after clashes with Hong Kong police]

https://www.thetimes.co.uk/edition/world/hong-kong-protesters-turn-ire-on-british-officers-after-clashes-with-police-over-china-extradition-bill-frg8b3hg3

After a crackdown on demonstrations this month, activists are calling for an inquiry into the tactics of the force and two UK veterans

Two senior British officers in the Hong Kong police force have become targets of angry protests for their roles as commanders during violent clashes over the territory’s now-shelved extradition bill.

During a blockade of police headquarters that ended early yesterday, some protesters carried pictures of the officers, Rupert Dover and David Jordan, with the message “Shame on HKPF”. In earlier protests “blood debt” was written in Chinese next to the pictures of the two Britons, both chief superintendents.

Demonstrators threw eggs and scrawled abusive graffiti on the police station walls over allegations of police brutality, while officers remained barricaded inside — very different tactics from the confrontations that took place during clashes around the Hong Kong legislature building on June 12.

The force did not respond to inquiries about the British officers, both long- serving veterans who joined before the handover to Chinese rule in 1997. But photographs and video seemed to confirm media reports that the men held senior command positions during the confrontations 11 days ago — as they did during the “umbrella movement” democracy protests of 2014.



Source: https://m.facebook.com/joshuawongchifung/

唔好俾佢停，借阻英國左膠力量

replies.msg:
Joshua Wong, a student leader arrested by officers led by Jordan during the 2014 showdown, said there was a “disappointing irony” to their roles.

“These officers come from Britain, a country where human rights, basic freedoms, justice and rule of law are taken for granted by its people,” he told The Sunday Times. “But that is what people here were demonstrating for when the police attacked them.”

Wong, who was released on Monday from his latest stint behind bars for the 2104 protests, urged Theresa May to raise the issue of Hong Kong police behaviour with Xi Jinping, the Chinese president, at next weekend’s G20 summit in Japan.

在1990年11月，保守黨黨魁約翰·梅傑接替柴契爾夫人出任首相一職，約翰·梅傑與彭定康交情甚好，因此他上任後隨即聘任彭定康為蘭卡斯特公爵領地總裁兼保守黨主席（繼黨魁、副黨魁後的第三號人物）。在彭定康的籌劃下，保守黨在1992年大選中意外地勝出，但諷刺的是，他本人卻被自由民主黨候選人唐·福斯特擊敗，喪失他在巴斯選區的下議院議席。據約翰·梅傑的自傳所講，若果當年彭定康能保住議席的話，將有機會出任財政大臣或外交大臣。from wiki

“These officers come from Britain, a country where human rights, basic freedoms, justice and rule of law are taken for granted by its people,” he told The Sunday Times. “But that is what people here were demonstrating for when the police attacked them.”

----------

[Protesters turn ire on British officers after clashes with Hong Kong police]

https://www.thetimes.co.uk/edition/world/hong-kong-protesters-turn-ire-on-british-officers-after-clashes-with-police-over-china-extradition-bill-frg8b3hg3

After a crackdown on demonstrations this month, activists are calling for an inquiry into the tactics of the force and two UK veterans

Two senior British officers in the Hong Kong police force have become targets of angry protests for their roles as commanders during violent clashes over the territory’s now-shelved extradition bill.

During a blockade of police headquarters that ended early yesterday, some protesters carried pictures of the officers, Rupert Dover and David Jordan, with the message “Shame on HKPF”. In earlier protests “blood debt” was written in Chinese next to the pictures of the two Britons, both chief superintendents.

Demonstrators threw eggs and scrawled abusive graffiti on the police station walls over allegations of police brutality, while officers remained barricaded inside — very different tactics from the confrontations that took place during clashes around the Hong Kong legislature building on June 12.

The force did not respond to inquiries about the British officers, both long- serving veterans who joined before the handover to Chinese rule in 1997. But photographs and video seemed to confirm media reports that the men held senior command positions during the confrontations 11 days ago — as they did during the “umbrella movement” democracy protests of 2014.



Source: https://m.facebook.com/joshuawongchifung/

唔好俾佢停，借阻英國左膠力量

Joshua Wong, a student leader arrested by officers led by Jordan during the 2014 showdown, said there was a “disappointing irony” to their roles.

“These officers come from Britain, a country where human rights, basic freedoms, justice and rule of law are taken for granted by its people,” he told The Sunday Times. “But that is what people here were demonstrating for when the police attacked them.”

Wong, who was released on Monday from his latest stint behind bars for the 2104 protests, urged Theresa May to raise the issue of Hong Kong police behaviour with Xi Jinping, the Chinese president, at next weekend’s G20 summit in Japan.

在1990年11月，保守黨黨魁約翰·梅傑接替柴契爾夫人出任首相一職，約翰·梅傑與彭定康交情甚好，因此他上任後隨即聘任彭定康為蘭卡斯特公爵領地總裁兼保守黨主席（繼黨魁、副黨魁後的第三號人物）。在彭定康的籌劃下，保守黨在1992年大選中意外地勝出，但諷刺的是，他本人卻被自由民主黨候選人唐·福斯特擊敗，喪失他在巴斯選區的下議院議席。據約翰·梅傑的自傳所講，若果當年彭定康能保住議席的話，將有機會出任財政大臣或外交大臣。from wiki
